briefing.headline: Rig up a Neko Rig — finesse fall approach for muddy water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Neko Rig is a strong choice for fall with muddy water and post frontal conditions.

Robertson: Pair a Neko rig (size 2 Gamakatsu circle hook, 6.5-inch finesse worm in green-pumpkin) with a Ned rig when targeting smallmouth on deep boulders. Work both presentations in a 60-foot area where fish are concentrated.

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.retrieve: Robertson: Pair a Neko rig (size 2 Gamakatsu circle hook, 6.5-inch finesse worm in green-pumpkin) with a Ned rig when targeting smallmouth on deep boulders. Work both presentations in a 60-foot area w
primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Wheeler: Use a Neko rig around docks when primary offshore spots get pressured — dock fish are often less beat up and can save a tournament late in the day
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, creek channel. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: In fall, fish school up in ditches, on little flats in 2-4 feet, and especially around grass; on grassless lakes, key on shallow ditches with stumps that let bass ambush shad.

Yamamoto: The Chicken Rig is weedless unlike the Neko rig so it can be fished through brush, rocks, and docks. It has big bass appeal with the large Kut Tail profile. It's durable because the worm slides up the line on hookset

KVD: 'Fall spinnerbaits: downsize to 3/16oz in clear water with small blades to match young-of-year baitfish. For heavy grass, go bigger with willowleaf blades. To fish fast and deep, put smaller blades on a 3/4oz head — the Scorcher design lets y...

Palaniuk: 'My offshore rotation goes: 1) deep-diving crankbait to cover water fast and fire up the school, 2) swimbait for a slower moving presentation once crankbait bite dies, 3) big flutter spoon for vertical reaction bites on fish seen on elec...

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal fall fish pull tighter to cover and deeper structure at 8-18ft. The shad migration pauses but doesn't stop. Focus on the deepest fish in a given creek arm — channel bends and main creek points.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If you find a cleaner pocket or tributary, switch to natural presentations — fish in cleaner water see more lures
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
